Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Megadev: A Development Kit for the Sega Mega Drive and Mega CD Hardware 101 upvotes, 5 comments
  2. Gimp 3.2 Released 48 upvotes, 2 comments
  3. Baochip-1x: What it is, why I'm doing it now and how it came about 238 upvotes, 33 comments
  4. Show HN: GitAgent – An open standard that turns any Git repo into an AI agent 66 upvotes, 6 comments
  5. Nmap in the movies (2008) 123 upvotes, 17 comments
  6. Generalizing Knuth's Pseudocode Architecture From Algorithms to Knowledge 16 upvotes, 0 comments
  7. Python: The Optimization Ladder 214 upvotes, 72 comments
  8. Cookie jars capture American kitsch (2023) 21 upvotes, 3 comments
  9. 1M context is now generally available for Opus 4.6 and Sonnet 4.6 1082 upvotes, 460 comments
  10. Sunsetting Jazzband 92 upvotes, 29 comments
  11. Philosoph Jürgen Habermas Gestorben 131 upvotes, 45 comments
  12. Show HN: Learn Arabic with spaced repetition and comprehensible input 41 upvotes, 11 comments
  13. Everything you never wanted to know about visually-hidden 22 upvotes, 5 comments
  14. In Praise of Stupid Questions 13 upvotes, 2 comments
  15. Show HN: Ichinichi – One note per day, E2E encrypted, local-first 19 upvotes, 5 comments
  16. Online astroturfing: A problem beyond disinformation (2022) 70 upvotes, 34 comments
  17. An ode to bzip 59 upvotes, 31 comments
  18. RAM kits are now sold with one fake RAM stick alongside a real one 224 upvotes, 150 comments
  19. Hostile Volume – A game about adjusting volume with intentionally bad UI 34 upvotes, 16 comments
  20. It's time to move your docs in the repo 40 upvotes, 20 comments
  21. Montana passes Right to Compute act (2025) 207 upvotes, 170 comments
  22. CSMWrap: Legacy BIOS booting on UEFI-only systems via SeaBIOS 8 upvotes, 2 comments
  23. What happens when US economic data becomes unreliable 266 upvotes, 234 comments
  24. 2026 tech layoffs reach 45,000 in March 41 upvotes, 27 comments
  25. XML Is a Cheap DSL 212 upvotes, 208 comments
  26. Life as an OnlyFans 'chatter' 98 upvotes, 88 comments
  27. Marketing for Founders 3 upvotes, 0 comments
  28. Starlink militarization and its impact on global strategic stability 77 upvotes, 98 comments
  29. UBI as a productivity dividend 81 upvotes, 138 comments
  30. 9 Mothers Defense (YC P26) Is Hiring in Austin 0 upvotes, 0 comments
